description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add a job named build that uses the juliend/drupal-build-tools-ci Docker image, runs `composer -n global require -n "hirak/prestissimo:^0.3"`, then `composer install --no-dev`, removes the .git directory with `rm -rf .git`, and stores the entire repository as an artifact named `plateform_${CI_BUILD_NAME}_${CI_BUILD_REF_NAME}_${CI_BUILD_REF}` that expires in one week. In the deploy stage add a manual job named Deploy:dev that runs only on the dev branch, sets up rsync and openssh-client if missing (`which rsync || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install rsync -y )` and `which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install openssh-client -y )`), starts ssh-agent (`eval $(ssh-agent -s)`), adds the private key (`ssh-add <(echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY")`), creates `~/.ssh/config` disabling strict host key checking, exports `TARGETPATH="${TARGETBASEPATH}"`, echoes host and path, writes a VERSION file with tag, build, ref, name and git describe, lists files, creates the remote directory via `ssh -p $TARGETPORT $TARGETHOST "mkdir -p $TARGETPATH"`, syncs the local `www` directory to `$TARGETHOST:$TARGETPATH` with `rsync -azv -e "ssh -p $TARGETPORT" --delete --exclude=/composer.* --exclude=/.ssh www $TARGETHOST:$TARGETPATH`, and finally echoes cleanup messages. The Deploy:dev job defines the environment name dev with URL `https://${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}.${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.ci-example.com`.
